intro: Hieracium binzii is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae), also known as hawkweed, which is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium binzii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium binzii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to exploit its purported health benefits.
